26.     )
27.     return {
28.         "cmd": cmd,
29.         "returncode": completed.returncode,
30.         "stdout": completed.stdout,
31.         "stderr": completed.stderr,
32.     }
33. 
34. 
35. def safe_version(binary: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
36.     path = shutil.which(binary)
37.     if not path:
38.         return {"path": None, "version": None}
39.     result = subprocess.run([path, "--version"], text=True, capture_output=True, check=False)
40.     version_line = (result.stdout or result.stderr).strip().splitlines()
41.     return {
42.         "path": path,
43.         "version": version_line[0] if version_line else None,
44.     }
45. 
46. 
47. def write_text(path: Path, text: str) -> None:
48.     path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
49.     path.write_text(text.rstrip() + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
50. 
51. 
52. def load_json(path: Path | None) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
53.     if path is None or not path.exists():
54.         return None
55.     return json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
56. 
57. 
58. def session_tool_name(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> str | None:
59.     data = payload.get("data")
60.     if not isinstance(data, dict):
61.         data = {}
62. 
63.     event_type = payload.get("type")
64.     if event_type == "tool.execution_start":
65.         # Count only execution_start so the paired completion event does not
66.         # double-count the same tool call in the current Copilot CLI schema.
67.         tool_name = data.get("toolName")
68.         if isinstance(tool_name, str) and tool_name:
69.             return tool_name
70. 
71.     # Legacy retained fixtures still expose tool names at the top level.
72.     # This runs only when the current-schema early return above did not match,
73.     # which keeps old replay tests working without counting nested hook
74.     # payloads from the current schema as tool calls.
75.     for candidate in (
76.         payload.get("tool_name"),
77.         payload.get("tool"),
78.         payload.get("recipient_name"),
79.         payload.get("name"),
80.     ):
81.         if isinstance(candidate, str) and candidate:
82.             return candidate
83.     return None

86. def nested_error_flag(value: Any, depth: int = 0, max_depth: int = 50) -> bool:
87.     """Look for explicit error fields only; depth bound keeps malformed input finite."""
88.     if depth >= max_depth:
89.         return False
90.     if isinstance(value, dict):
91.         for key, nested in value.items():
92.             key_text = str(key).lower()
93.             if key_text == "level" and isinstance(nested, str) and nested.lower() == "error":
94.                 return True
95.             if key_text in {"error", "errors"}:
96.                 if isinstance(nested, bool):
97.                     if nested:
98.                         return True
99.                 elif nested not in (None, "", [], {}):
100.                     return True

def parse_phase1b_items(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
    """Parse canonical Phase 1B interim bullets into normalized item records.

    Expected input uses `##`/`###` headings and bullets of the form
    `- **Title** — body [links]`. Trailing link bundles are stripped from the
    description and preserved separately in `sources`.
    """
    items: list[dict[str, object]] = []
    section = ""
    subsection = ""

    for raw_line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
        line = raw_line.rstrip()
        if line.startswith("## "):
            section = line[3:].strip()
            subsection = ""
            continue
        if line.startswith("### "):
            subsection = line[4:].strip()
            continue

        match = BULLET_ITEM.match(line)
        if not match:
            continue

        title = match.group(1).strip()
        body = match.group(2).strip()
        links = extract_links(body)
        if links:
            body = re.sub(
                r"\s*(?:\[[^\]]+\]\([^)]+\))(?:\s*\|\s*\[[^\]]+\]\([^)]+\))*\s*$",
                "",
                body,
            ).strip()
        item_section = subsection or section or path.stem

        items.append(
            build_item(
                section=item_section,
                title=title,
                description=body,
                sources=links,
                date=extract_date_hint(section),
                ide_support=infer_ide_support(f"{path.stem} {title} {body}"),
                relevance_score=infer_relevance(body, item_section),
            )
        )

    return items

        if line.startswith("## "):
            section = line[3:].strip()
            subsection = ""
            continue
        if line.startswith("### "):
            subsection = line[4:].strip()
            continue

        match = BULLET_ITEM.match(line)
        if not match:
            continue

        title = match.group(1).strip()
        body = match.group(2).strip()
        links = extract_links(body)
        if links:
            body = re.sub(
                r"\s*(?:\[[^\]]+\]\([^)]+\))(?:\s*\|\s*\[[^\]]+\]\([^)]+\))*\s*$",
                "",
                body,
            ).strip()
        item_section = subsection or section or path.stem

        items.append(
            build_item(
                section=item_section,
                title=title,
                description=body,
                sources=links,
                date=extract_date_hint(section),
                ide_support=infer_ide_support(f"{path.stem} {title} {body}"),
                relevance_score=infer_relevance(body, item_section),
            )
        )

    return items


def parse_discovery_items(path: Path) -> list[dict[str, object]]:
    return dedupe_items(parse_markdown_items(path) + parse_inline_discovery_items(path))


def extract_declared_total_discoveries(path: Path) -> int | None:
    for raw_line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
        match = DECLARED_TOTAL_DISCOVERIES.match(raw_line.strip())
        if match:
            return int(match.group(1))
    return None


def count_candidate_discovery_lines(path: Path) -> int:
    total = 0
    for raw_line in path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines():
        line = raw_line.rstrip()
        if any(pattern.match(line) for pattern in CANDIDATE_DISCOVERY_PATTERNS):
            total += 1
    return total
